system_prompt: You are a research assistant. Use the wikipedia tools to answer the user's question. When you have the answer, call finish(answer=...).
user_prompt: What Kenya-based company owns the Tusker beer brand?

tool_schemas.name: search_wikipedia
tool_schemas.description: Search Wikipedia for page titles matching a query. Returns up to 5 {title, snippet} results.

tool_schemas.name: get_wiki_page
tool_schemas.description: Fetch a Wikipedia page by exact title. Returns {title, summary, text}. If the page does not exist, returns {error: 'not_found', title}.

tool_schemas.name: finish
tool_schemas.description: Submit the final short answer to the user's question.

text:
<think>
Okay, the user is asking which Kenya-based company owns the Tusker beer brand. Let me start by figuring out how to find this information. Since I can use Wikipedia tools, I should first search for "Tusker beer" to see if there's a relevant page.

I'll call the search_wikipedia function with the query "Tusker beer". The results might include a page about the beer brand itself or its parent company. Let me check the first few results. 

Assuming the first result is the Tusker beer Wikipedia page, I can then use get_wiki_page to fetch the full text. If that page mentions the parent company, great. If not, maybe I need to search for the company name. Alternatively, if the search results include a company page that owns Tusker, that would be better. 

Wait, maybe the company is called Tusker Brewery? Let me check. If the initial search doesn't give the company, I might need to search for "Tusker Brewery" or look for Kenyan breweries. Another approach: search for "Kenya breweries" and see if Tusker is listed there. 

Alternatively, the answer might be that Tusker is owned by the Kenya Breweries Limited, but I need to confirm. Let me first get the initial search results for "Tusker beer".
</think>

<tool_call>
{"name": "search_wikipedia", "arguments": {"query": "Tusker beer"}}
</tool_call><|im_end|>

<think>
Okay, the user is asking which Kenya-based company owns the Tusker beer brand. Let me start by looking at the search results from the Wikipedia query.

The first result is "Tusker (beer)", which seems relevant. The snippet is empty, so I need to fetch the full page. Let me use get_wiki_page with the title "Tusker (beer)" to get more details. 

If that page doesn't mention the owner, maybe the next steps are to search for Kenyan breweries or look into the company's history. But let's check the Tusker beer page first. 

Alternatively, the second result is "Bert Tucker", but that's a person, not a company. Similarly, the others like Beverley D. Tucker and Bernard Tucker are also individuals. The Tuskegee Railroad is unrelated. So the most promising is the "Tusker (beer)" page. 

Once I get the summary or text from that page, I can look for information about the parent company. If it's not there, maybe I need to search for "Kenya Breweries" or similar terms. But let's proceed with the Tusker (beer) page first.
</think>

<tool_call>
{"name": "get_wiki_page", "arguments": {"title": "Tusker (beer)"}}
</tool_call><|im_end|>

tool_results.summary: Tusker or Tuskers may refer to:
tool_results.text:
Tusker or Tuskers may refer to:


== Animals ==
an elephant with large tusks
an adult male wild boar, particularly the American razorback population


== Food and drink ==
Tusker (African beer), a Kenyan beer bottled by East African Breweries
Tusker (Vanuatu beer), a beer brewed by Vanuatu Brewing in Vanuatu


== Military unit nicknames ==
4th Battalion, 64th Armor Regiment, a United States Army unit
No. 5 Squadron IAF, an Indian Air Force squadron
413 Transport and Rescue Squadron, a Canadian Armed Forces air force squadron


== Sports teams ==


=== Cricket ===
Ballari Tuskers, a Karnataka Premier League cricket franchise
Kandy Tuskers, a team in the Lanka Premier League now known as Kandy Falcons
Kochi Tuskers Kerala, a former Indian Premier League cricket franchise
Matabeleland Tuskers, a Zimbabwean cricket franchise
Tuskers women's cricket team, a Zimbabwean women's cricket team
Karnataka Tuskers, a team in the 2019 Abu Dhabi T10


=== Other sports ===
Sri Lanka rugby union team, nicknamed Tuskers
East Africa rugby union team, who tour under the nickname Tuskers
Tusker F.C., a Kenyan football club owned by the East African Breweries
Florida Tuskers, a former United Football League franchise


== Other uses ==
Tusker Rock, a rock in the Bristol Channel, Wales
a character in the Japanese anime series Kiba
a member of the DC Comics Justice League of Earth
the University of Nebraska's former Holland Computing Center high memory cluster
the name of a DHR B Class steam locomotive
the sports teams of Somers High School (New York), a public school


== See also ==
Tusk (disambiguation)
tool_results.title: Tusker

text:
<think>
Okay, the user is asking which Kenya-based company owns the Tusker beer brand. Let me look through the information I have.

From the Wikipedia search, the first result was "Tusker (beer)", and when I fetched that page, the text mentions "Tusker (African beer), a Kenyan beer bottled by East African Breweries". So that seems like the key point here. The summary also lists "Tusker (African beer), a Kenyan beer bottled by East African Breweries" under the Food and drink section. 

I need to confirm if East African Breweries is the company. The page also mentions other entries like Tusker F.C., a Kenyan football club owned by the East African Breweries. That reinforces that East African Breweries is the owner. 

So the answer should be East African Breweries Limited. Let me make sure there's no other conflicting information. The other entries are about different things like animals, military units, etc., so they don't interfere. 

Therefore, the Kenya-based company is East African Breweries Limited.
</think>

<tool_call>
{"name": "finish", "arguments": {"answer": "The Tusker beer brand is owned by East African Breweries Limited, a Kenya-based company."}}
</tool_call><|im_end|>

arguments.answer: The Tusker beer brand is owned by East African Breweries Limited, a Kenya-based company.

tool_results.answer: The Tusker beer brand is owned by East African Breweries Limited, a Kenya-based company.
